segments.text: This plate costs three times more than a plastic one and you should absolutely buy it because this is not just a plate.
It's the answer to the problem that we've ignored for decades. See, normal plates are a disaster. We drill oil to make plastic, shape it into plate, use
it for just 15 minutes and then throw it away. Then that cheap plate sits in landfill for forever poisoning the soil.
So we found a solution in nature's trash. When we crush sugarcane for juice, we are left with this dry fiber called bugass. Usually this is just burned which creates massive air
pollution. So instead of burning it, we mash it, mold it, and bake it into these. Unlike flimsy plastic, these feel premium. They're rock solid, leakproof,
and you can even microwave them. But the best part, when you're done, you don't create trash. You just bury it. In about
90 days, it breaks down completely and becomes food for the soil. Expensive maybe, but worth it? Definitely.
